rules.description: 2D barcode (PDF417 or DataMatrix) encoding shipment data including tracking, origin, destination, and service type for automated processing.
The full recipient/consignee address block on the ESS Label, subject to GDPR restrictions.
The city of the shipment destination/receiver within the consignee address block.
The contact person name at the destination, recommended when the consignee is a company.
The destination country as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code or full country name within the consignee address block.
The name of the person or company to whom the shipment is being delivered, prominently displayed in the destination address block.
The telephone number of the receiver/consignee for delivery contact and customs clearance, required for international shipments.
The postal/ZIP code of the shipment destination, a critical sortation element used to determine destination service area routing.
The state, province, or region of the shipment destination, required where applicable by country.
The full shipper/consignor address block on the ESS Label, subject to GDPR restrictions.
Shipper contact details, no longer required on the Transport Label except in exceptional regulatory circumstances such as UN3373 compliance.
Mandatory field describing the contents of the shipment, up to 300 characters, or 'CTD – see data' for approved neutral content descriptions.
Optional region-specific routing codes within the routing barcode for domestic use only, providing additional sortation information.
ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency used for the declared value of service.
Optional customer logo or DHL Initiative Logo (e.g., GoGreen) placed to the right of the sender address segment.
A 2D Data Matrix barcode per ISO/IEC 16022 encoding operational or customer data on 2D-enabled Transport Label layouts.
A 2D Data Matrix barcode containing customer-specific data on 2D-enabled transport labels per ISO/IEC 16022.
Delivery date display showing day of month (01-31) or special codes (A=appointment, S=Saturday, H=hold at depot).
A 2-digit code in the routing barcode indicating the delivery date or scheduling option (00=no date, 01-31=day, 50=Saturday, 51=appointment, 52=hold).
Delivery time window code displayed on label (e.g., X09, X10, X12) with a single-digit code in the routing barcode.
Single-digit numeric code indicating the delivery time feature (0=none, 1=pre-09:00, 2=pre-12:00, 3=pre-10:30)
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the shipment destination, displayed prominently for sortation
DHL facility code for the delivery facility, consisting of country code, service area code, and optional facility code
Postcode of the consignee address encoded in the routing barcode, max 12 characters with no spaces or symbols
Three-character code identifying the DHL service area at the destination responsible for final delivery
Official DHL carrier logo printed in the upper right corner of the transport label
Three-digit handling feature codes representing product features impacting sorting and delivery, displayed in reverse video
Visual logos/icons indicating specific handling-relevant services such as Medical Express
Sort code used at destination inbound facilities for manual sorting, up to 4 alphanumeric characters
ISO 3166-1 destination country code encoded in the routing barcode, 2-alpha (ASC MH10) or 3-digit (GS1) format
Unique identifier assigned by the GCoCL team certifying the transport label is approved for production use
Date the label was printed in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-mm-dd)
Heart shape logo with pulse line identifying Medical Express shipments requiring highest priority handling
Three-character code identifying the DHL service area at the origin that processes the shipment
Sort code for manual sorting at outbound facilities, up to 4 alphanumeric characters or dot if no value found
Relative piece number and total pieces in shipment in X/Y format, with dot replacing unknown values
Unique piece identifier (license plate) per ISO/IEC 15459-1 with JD01 data identifier prefix, encoded in Code 128 barcode
Sequential number identifying this specific piece within a multi-piece shipment
Customer-defined alphanumeric reference identifier for the piece or shipment, qualified by Reference Type Codes
Weight of an individual piece/package, mandatory for Remote Pickup shipments and printed when available for multi-piece shipments
Graphical weight warning icons (10+ kg or 20+ kg) required for shipments delivered in Germany per federal law
Identifies the label printing/rendering application and its version number in X.XX.XXX format
2-digit numeric code representing the DHL transport product type, encoded within the routing barcode
DHL's internal 3-character uppercase code for the product under which a transport order is processed
Global Product Short Name identifying the DHL Express product sold to the customer, up to 20 uppercase characters
Short code identifying a value-added service associated with the shipment, displayed when applicable
QR Code barcode encoding operational data for PUD and in-facility domains, used in LabelFree scenarios
Customer-defined shipper reference number for cross-referencing with their own systems
Additional customer-defined reference number for the shipment
Legally required regulatory text such as country-specific declarations, export info, or dangerous goods declarations
Barcode containing the DHL ISO Routing Code encoding destination country, postcode, product code, and features in Code 128 or GS1-128 format
Composite sortation code combining destination service area code, facility code, and sort indicators displayed prominently on the label
Human-readable text of the routing barcode content with parenthesized data identifier, shown adjacent to the routing barcode
3-character uppercase letter code representing the locally defined DHL service area of origin
Visual icon representing the DHL service type for quick identification of service level by handlers
Human-readable name of the DHL Express service/product used for the shipment
1-3 character alphanumeric code identifying the DHL shipping service/product selected for the shipment
Sender/consignor's multi-line address block with 'From' header, max 7 lines at 47 characters each
Consignee's delivery address block with 'To' header, max 7 lines, emphasized with hooks and larger font for country and postal code
The date the shipment is tendered to DHL, typically in DD-MMM-YYYY format.
DHL 10-digit waybill number consisting of 9 base digits plus a MOD 7 check digit.
The sum of weights of all pieces in the shipment, displayed with unit of measure (kg or lb).
The 9-digit DHL account number of the shipper used for billing and identification.
The full street address of the shipment origin/sender including street, city, state, postal code, and country.
The name of the person or company sending the shipment.
The city of the shipment origin/sender within the shipper address block.
The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the shipment origin/sender.
The telephone number of the shipper/sender for contact purposes.
The postal/ZIP code of the shipment origin, format varies by country.
The state, province, or region of the shipment origin/sender where applicable.
The primary DHL tracking/waybill number used to track the package through the DHL network.
1D barcode (Code 128) encoding the DHL tracking/waybill number for automated scanning.
Code 39 barcode encoding the 10-digit waybill number with MOD 7 check digit.
2D Data Matrix ECC 200 barcode encoding operational data per ISO/IEC 15434 with public, open, and customer segments.
Additional special or conditional information appearing on the label based on specific shipment requirements.
The declared monetary value associated with a value-added service such as insured delivery.
The actual weight of the shipment or piece displayed on the label in kg or lbs.
A visual icon printed when piece weight is 10 kg (22 lb) or above to alert handlers to heavier packages.
Unit of measurement for shipment weight, indicating kilograms (K/KG) or pounds (L/LB)

2d_barcode.description: 2D barcode (PDF417 or DataMatrix) encoding shipment data including tracking, origin, destination, and service type for automated processing.

consignee_address.description: The full recipient/consignee address block on the ESS Label, subject to GDPR restrictions.

consignee_city.description: The city of the shipment destination/receiver within the consignee address block.

consignee_contact_name.description: The contact person name at the destination, recommended when the consignee is a company.

consignee_country.description: The destination country as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code or full country name within the consignee address block.

consignee_name.description: The name of the person or company to whom the shipment is being delivered, prominently displayed in the destination address block.

consignee_phone.description: The telephone number of the receiver/consignee for delivery contact and customs clearance, required for international shipments.

consignee_postal_code.description: The postal/ZIP code of the shipment destination, a critical sortation element used to determine destination service area routing.

consignee_state_province.description: The state, province, or region of the shipment destination, required where applicable by country.

consignor_address.description: The full shipper/consignor address block on the ESS Label, subject to GDPR restrictions.

consignor_contact.description: Shipper contact details, no longer required on the Transport Label except in exceptional regulatory circumstances such as UN3373 compliance.

content_description.description: Mandatory field describing the contents of the shipment, up to 300 characters, or 'CTD – see data' for approved neutral content descriptions.

country_specific_codes.description: Optional region-specific routing codes within the routing barcode for domestic use only, providing additional sortation information.

currency_code.description: ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency used for the declared value of service.

customer_logo.description: Optional customer logo or DHL Initiative Logo (e.g., GoGreen) placed to the right of the sender address segment.

data_matrix_barcode.description: A 2D Data Matrix barcode per ISO/IEC 16022 encoding operational or customer data on 2D-enabled Transport Label layouts.

data_matrix_customer_data.description: A 2D Data Matrix barcode containing customer-specific data on 2D-enabled transport labels per ISO/IEC 16022.

delivery_date.description: Delivery date display showing day of month (01-31) or special codes (A=appointment, S=Saturday, H=hold at depot).

delivery_date_code.description: A 2-digit code in the routing barcode indicating the delivery date or scheduling option (00=no date, 01-31=day, 50=Saturday, 51=appointment, 52=hold).

delivery_time.description: Delivery time window code displayed on label (e.g., X09, X10, X12) with a single-digit code in the routing barcode.

delivery_time_code.description: Single-digit numeric code indicating the delivery time feature (0=none, 1=pre-09:00, 2=pre-12:00, 3=pre-10:30)

destination_country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the shipment destination, displayed prominently for sortation

destination_facility_code.description: DHL facility code for the delivery facility, consisting of country code, service area code, and optional facility code

destination_postcode.description: Postcode of the consignee address encoded in the routing barcode, max 12 characters with no spaces or symbols

destination_service_area_code.description: Three-character code identifying the DHL service area at the destination responsible for final delivery

dhl_logo.description: Official DHL carrier logo printed in the upper right corner of the transport label

handling_feature_codes.description: Three-digit handling feature codes representing product features impacting sorting and delivery, displayed in reverse video

handling_service_logos.description: Visual logos/icons indicating specific handling-relevant services such as Medical Express

inbound_sort_code.description: Sort code used at destination inbound facilities for manual sorting, up to 4 alphanumeric characters

iso_country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 destination country code encoded in the routing barcode, 2-alpha (ASC MH10) or 3-digit (GS1) format

label_certification_identifier.description: Unique identifier assigned by the GCoCL team certifying the transport label is approved for production use

label_printing_date.description: Date the label was printed in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-mm-dd)

medical_express_logo.description: Heart shape logo with pulse line identifying Medical Express shipments requiring highest priority handling

origin_service_area_code.description: Three-character code identifying the DHL service area at the origin that processes the shipment

outbound_sort_code.description: Sort code for manual sorting at outbound facilities, up to 4 alphanumeric characters or dot if no value found

piece_count.description: Relative piece number and total pieces in shipment in X/Y format, with dot replacing unknown values

piece_identifier.description: Unique piece identifier (license plate) per ISO/IEC 15459-1 with JD01 data identifier prefix, encoded in Code 128 barcode

piece_number.description: Sequential number identifying this specific piece within a multi-piece shipment

piece_shipment_reference.description: Customer-defined alphanumeric reference identifier for the piece or shipment, qualified by Reference Type Codes

piece_weight.description: Weight of an individual piece/package, mandatory for Remote Pickup shipments and printed when available for multi-piece shipments

piece_weight_symbols.description: Graphical weight warning icons (10+ kg or 20+ kg) required for shipments delivered in Germany per federal law

printing_application.description: Identifies the label printing/rendering application and its version number in X.XX.XXX format

product_code.description: 2-digit numeric code representing the DHL transport product type, encoded within the routing barcode

product_content_code.description: DHL's internal 3-character uppercase code for the product under which a transport order is processed

product_name.description: Global Product Short Name identifying the DHL Express product sold to the customer, up to 20 uppercase characters

product_service_code.description: Short code identifying a value-added service associated with the shipment, displayed when applicable

qr_code.description: QR Code barcode encoding operational data for PUD and in-facility domains, used in LabelFree scenarios

reference_number.description: Customer-defined shipper reference number for cross-referencing with their own systems

reference_number_2.description: Additional customer-defined reference number for the shipment

regulatory_information.description: Legally required regulatory text such as country-specific declarations, export info, or dangerous goods declarations

routing_barcode.description: Barcode containing the DHL ISO Routing Code encoding destination country, postcode, product code, and features in Code 128 or GS1-128 format

routing_code.description: Composite sortation code combining destination service area code, facility code, and sort indicators displayed prominently on the label

routing_code_human_readable.description: Human-readable text of the routing barcode content with parenthesized data identifier, shown adjacent to the routing barcode

service_area_code_of_origin.description: 3-character uppercase letter code representing the locally defined DHL service area of origin

service_icon.description: Visual icon representing the DHL service type for quick identification of service level by handlers

service_name.description: Human-readable name of the DHL Express service/product used for the shipment

service_type.description: 1-3 character alphanumeric code identifying the DHL shipping service/product selected for the shipment

ship_from_address.description: Sender/consignor's multi-line address block with 'From' header, max 7 lines at 47 characters each

ship_to_address.description: Consignee's delivery address block with 'To' header, max 7 lines, emphasized with hooks and larger font for country and postal code

shipment_date.description: The date the shipment is tendered to DHL, typically in DD-MMM-YYYY format.

shipment_identifier.description: DHL 10-digit waybill number consisting of 9 base digits plus a MOD 7 check digit.

shipment_weight.description: The sum of weights of all pieces in the shipment, displayed with unit of measure (kg or lb).

shipper_account_number.description: The 9-digit DHL account number of the shipper used for billing and identification.

shipper_address.description: The full street address of the shipment origin/sender including street, city, state, postal code, and country.

shipper_name.description: The name of the person or company sending the shipment.

shipper_city.description: The city of the shipment origin/sender within the shipper address block.

shipper_country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the shipment origin/sender.

shipper_phone.description: The telephone number of the shipper/sender for contact purposes.

shipper_postal_code.description: The postal/ZIP code of the shipment origin, format varies by country.

shipper_state_province.description: The state, province, or region of the shipment origin/sender where applicable.

tracking_number.description: The primary DHL tracking/waybill number used to track the package through the DHL network.

tracking_number_barcode.description: 1D barcode (Code 128) encoding the DHL tracking/waybill number for automated scanning.

waybill_number_barcode.description: Code 39 barcode encoding the 10-digit waybill number with MOD 7 check digit.

two_d_data_matrix.description: 2D Data Matrix ECC 200 barcode encoding operational data per ISO/IEC 15434 with public, open, and customer segments.

special_conditional_information.description: Additional special or conditional information appearing on the label based on specific shipment requirements.

value_of_service.description: The declared monetary value associated with a value-added service such as insured delivery.

weight.description: The actual weight of the shipment or piece displayed on the label in kg or lbs.

weight_icon.description: A visual icon printed when piece weight is 10 kg (22 lb) or above to alert handlers to heavier packages.

weight_unit.description: Unit of measurement for shipment weight, indicating kilograms (K/KG) or pounds (L/LB)
